steps.title: Use the equal sides
steps.content: Since side RT equals side TU, triangle RTU is isosceles. The two base angles at R and U have the same measure. Let each base angle be $t^{\circ}$.

steps.title: Find the base angle
steps.content:
The angle at T is $114^{\circ}$, so the triangle angle sum gives
$$
114^{\circ}+2t^{\circ}=180^{\circ}
$$
$$
2t^{\circ}=66^{\circ}
$$
$$
t=33
$$
Thus the angle at U in triangle SUV is also $33^{\circ}$.

steps.title: Use the exterior angle
steps.content: The exterior angle labeled $x^{\circ}$ equals the sum of the two opposite interior angles of triangle SUV: $$ x^{\circ}=31^{\circ}+33^{\circ}=64^{\circ} $$ The answer is C.
